# opentools

> opentools is a collection of fast, private image, PDF, GIF, color, metadata, and file utilities. Processing happens locally in the user's web browser; files are not uploaded to an opentools server.

Canonical site: https://opentools.fun/
Language: English
Access: Free, no account required

## Primary directories

- [Image tools](https://opentools.fun/image): Browse image tools grouped by conversion, resizing, compression, editing, organization, web design, metadata, mobile, GIF, color, and miscellaneous tasks.
- [PDF tools](https://opentools.fun/pdf): Browse tools for creating PDFs, rendering PDF pages as images, and extracting images from PDFs.
- [Image converter](https://opentools.fun/convert): Convert images among JPEG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, TIFF, GIF, BMP, ICO, and other formats.

## Notable capabilities

- Convert, resize, crop, rotate, flip, compress, and batch-process images.
- Add text, watermarks, borders, rounded corners, and visual effects.
- Merge images, split images, create collages, and package images into ZIP files.
- Create PDFs from images, convert PDF pages to images, and extract embedded PDF images.
- Create and process animated GIFs.
- Inspect or remove EXIF metadata and change image DPI.
- Pick colors, extract palettes, convert RGB and HEX values, and count image colors.
- Rename files, generate favicons and thumbnails, and create Base64 or data URI output.

## Privacy and operation

- Uploaded files remain on the user's device.
- Image and document processing is performed client-side using browser APIs and lazily loaded codecs.
- No account or sign-in is required to use the tools.
- Tool availability and supported formats are documented on each tool page.

## Crawling

- [Sitemap](https://opentools.fun/sitemap.xml)
- [Robots policy](https://opentools.fun/robots.txt)
